skill.name: golang-pro
skill.description: Master Go 1.21+ with modern patterns, advanced concurrency, performance optimization, and production-ready microservices
skill.summary: Expert Go development assistant for Go 1.21+ with advanced concurrency patterns and production-ready architecture
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected no security issues. No suspicious patterns, risk factors, or malicious intent found. The skill provides Go development guidance through instructions only.

content.user_title: Build Production-Ready Go Services with Advanced Concurrency
content.value_statement: Building high-performance Go applications requires mastering complex concurrency patterns and modern Go 1.21+ features. This skill provides expert guidance for production-ready microservices, performance optimization, and comprehensive testing strategies.
actual_capabilities: Design and implement concurrent systems using goroutines, channels, and sync primitives
Build production-ready microservices with gRPC, REST APIs, and WebSocket support
Optimize Go application performance using profiling, benchmarking, and memory analysis
Implement comprehensive testing strategies including table-driven tests and integration tests
Set up modern Go development tooling including workspaces, linting, and CI/CD pipelines
Design cloud-native Go applications with observability and containerization
limitations: Cannot execute or build Go code directly - provides guidance and code patterns only
Requires external Go tooling and runtime environment for actual code execution
Knowledge focused on Go 1.21+ - may not cover very recent releases or experimental features
Does not replace dedicated DevOps tools for deployment and infrastructure management

use_cases.title: Build High-Performance Microservices
use_cases.description: Develop scalable Go microservices with proper concurrency patterns, error handling, and observability for production deployment

use_cases.title: Optimize Go Application Performance
use_cases.description: Profile and optimize existing Go code for better throughput, memory usage, and latency using pprof and benchmarking

use_cases.title: Learn Modern Go Concurrency Patterns
use_cases.description: Master advanced concurrency patterns including worker pools, pipelines, and fan-in/fan-out for concurrent system design
use_cases.target_user: Developers learning Go or advancing their Go skills

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Go Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Go REST API server with proper error handling, structured logging, and graceful shutdown
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new Go web service project

prompt_templates.title: Implement Concurrent Worker Pool
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a worker pool pattern that processes jobs concurrently with configurable workers and graceful shutdown

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Go Code Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Go code for performance bottlenecks using profiling and suggest optimizations for memory and CPU usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Improving performance of existing Go applications

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Production-Ready Go Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a complete Go project with Docker, CI/CD pipeline, comprehensive testing, and observability including metrics and tracing

output_examples.input: Design a worker pool for processing image uploads concurrently
output_examples.output: A complete Go worker pool implementation with buffered channels for job distribution, configurable worker count, context-based cancellation, graceful shutdown handling, and proper error aggregation with wait groups and mutex protection

output_examples.input: Optimize this Go HTTP handler for better performance
output_examples.output: Performance analysis report identifying bottlenecks such as excessive memory allocations, missing connection pooling, or inefficient JSON encoding, with specific code improvements using sync.Pool, buffered I/O, and streaming responses

output_examples.input: Implement a gRPC service with proper error handling
output_examples.output: A complete gRPC service definition with protocol buffers, interceptor middleware for error handling and logging, context propagation for deadlines, and comprehensive unit tests with table-driven test patterns

best_practices: Always use context for cancellation and timeouts in concurrent operations and network calls
Prefer channels and select statements over shared memory with mutexes for goroutine communication
Write table-driven tests for comprehensive coverage and use benchmarks before optimizing performance
anti_patterns: Avoid creating goroutines without proper lifecycle management or cancellation mechanisms
Do not use defer inside loops for resource cleanup as it delays execution until function return
Never ignore errors or use blank identifier for returns without explicit error handling rationale

faq.question: What Go version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill specializes in Go 1.21+ features including generics, improved type inference, slog structured logging, and workspace support for multi-module development.

faq.question: Can this skill execute Go code?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides expert guidance, code patterns, and architectural advice for Go development. You need a local Go installation and tooling to execute and build the code.

faq.question: What concurrency patterns are covered?
faq.answer: Covers goroutine lifecycle management, channel patterns including fan-in/fan-out and pipelines, worker pools, select statements, context cancellation, mutex usage, and atomic operations for lock-free programming.

faq.question: Does this skill help with performance optimization?
faq.answer: Yes, provides expertise in CPU and memory profiling with pprof, benchmark-driven optimization, garbage collection tuning, memory leak detection, and caching strategies for production workloads.

faq.question: What testing strategies are recommended?
faq.answer: Advocates table-driven tests, comprehensive unit testing with testify, integration testing with test containers, benchmark tests for performance regression detection, and property-based testing with gopter.

faq.question: Can this skill help with microservices architecture?
faq.answer: Yes, covers microservices patterns, gRPC and REST API design, event-driven architecture with message queues, CQRS patterns, service mesh integration, and cloud-native deployment with Kubernetes and observability.
